scope_note: QA Witt Tower Doubly-Ramified Period Law cert [438]. [437] analysed the e=1 case for simple ramification v_p(t-2)=1. This cert extends the analysis to v_p(t-2)=2 (p^2 divides t-2 exactly). The orbit structure changes qualitatively: count(1,k)=p at k=1 (same as v_p=1) but JUMPS to p^2 for all k>=2, reflecting double nilpotency of N=M-I mod p^2. Full orbit law for v_p=2, k>=2: period set={p^L:L=0..k}; count(1)=p^2; count(p^L,1<=L<=k-2)=(p^2-1)*p^L (frozen); count(p^(k-1))=count(p^k)=(p-1)*p^(k-1) (joint birth -- top two layers equal). Mechanism: N^2=(t-2)*M=p^2*c2*M exactly (integer identity, same as [437]/[436]/[435] chain); when p^2|(t-2), N^2≡0 mod p^2 (doubly nilpotent, stronger than [437]'s N^2≡0 mod p); ker(N mod p^2)={(a,a):a in Z/p^2Z}=p^2 elements (vs p elements for v_p=1). No p=3 exception: formula holds for all p>=3 and all c2=(t-2)/p^2 with no stall. Joint birth: the top two orbit layers (L=k-1 and L=k) carry the same count, reflecting that the doubled ramification gives one extra free lifting step -- the tower cannot distinguish birth at L=k-1 from birth at L=k. Primary sources: Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (higher ramification groups, doubly-ramified extensions); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7 (Hensel lifting, nilpotent elements mod p^k); Wall, D.D. (1960) American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano-period structure for quadratic recurrences). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (C1) V2_PERIOD_SET: period set={p^L:L=0..k} for p=5, c2=1..4, k=1..4, zero violations PASS. (C2) V2_FIXED_JUMP: count(1,k)=p at k=1, count(1,k)=p^2 for k>=2; p=3,5,7, all c2, k=1..4; zero failures PASS. (C3) V2_ORBIT_FORMULA: full formula verified p=3,5,7, all c2 in {1..p-1}, k=1..4, zero violations PASS. (C4) DOUBLE_NILPOTENT: N^2=(t-2)*M exact integer t=3..299; N^2≡0 mod p^2 for all p^2|(t-2); ker(N mod p^2)=p^2 verified; zero failures PASS. (C5) NO_P3_EXCEPTION: p=3, c2=1 and c2=2 both pass formula at k=1..5; no stall analogue; PASS. (C6) K1_INVISIBLE: v_p=2 and v_p=1 give identical k=1 distributions for all p; PASS. WHY NOT DUPLICATE [437]: [437] analyses v_p=1; this cert analyses v_p=2 with structurally different fixed-point count (p vs p^2), different frozen formula ((p^2-1)*p^L vs (p^2-1)*p^(L-1)), and distinct joint-birth phenomenon (two equal top layers). THEOREM NT: all arithmetic pure integer mod p^k or exact integer identity; no float, no observer projection. 6 checks PASS; 7/7 fixtures PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-17.
